title: Uniface 10.4.01: Repository Changes
text: Uniface 10.4 .01: Repository Changes Changes have been made to the Uniface Repository that will allow new features to be added to the Uniface IDE, such as fine-grained version control for library objects, and searching and filtering on resources. The following fields have been added to all *.DICT entities: UKEYWORDS (W256)—for future enhancements. The following fields have been added to *.DICT entities for sub-objects. They are already available for main development objects. UPURPOSE (C1)—for future enhancements. UTIMESTAMP (E)—for future enhancements. The following fields have been enlarged: UDESCR (W256)—description of the object; increased from 64 to 256 Unicode characters. For more information, see Description . UALT_NAME (W128)—descriptive name for the object, which can include formatting characters; increased from 64 to 128 Unicode characters. For more information, see Alternative Name . CAUTION: Do not run Uniface 10.4 against an earlier Repository, and do not use earlier meta model definitions in Uniface 10.3. Related Links Uniface 10: Repository Changes To support the new Uniface IDE, changes have been made to the Uniface Repository. This means that it is not possible to run the Uniface IDE against a Uniface 9 Repository or even a Uniface 10.3 Repository, so you must migrate your application sources to the new Repository. The changes have no impact on runtime behavior. Uniface 10: Repository Changes per Entity This topic lists the new, changed, and deleted fields for each Repository entity. Was this topic helpful?
